verses.commentary: Therefore connects doctrine (chapters 1-11) to practice (chapters 12-16). In view of God's mercy motivates ethics. Living sacrifice contrasts with dead animals. We offer ourselves, alive and ongoing. Holy and pleasing describes acceptable worship. True and proper worship (logikē latreia, λογικὴ λατρεία, spiritual/reasonable service) is not ritual but self-offering.

Negative: do not conform (syschēmatizō, συσχηματίζω, be molded by). Positive: be transformed (metamorphoō, μεταμορφόω, undergo metamorphosis). Transformation comes through mind renewal. The renewed mind discerns God's will: good, pleasing, and perfect. Christian living flows from transformed thinking.

Do not think more highly (hyperphroneō, ὑπερφρονέω) warns against pride. Sober judgment (sōphroneō, σωφρονέω) means sound thinking. Faith is distributed by God in measure. This prevents boasting; all is grace.
One body, many members. Each member has a distinct function. Diversity is by design.
Though many, we are united. Each member belongs to all the others (mutual belonging). This creates interdependence. No member is independent.

They differ according to God's distribution. Prophesying (prophēteia, προφητεία) is declaring God's word. Exercise it in accordance with faith (the faith, or the measure of faith). Gifts must align with sound doctrine.
Teaching (didaskalia, διδασκαλία) is instruction. Each gift should be exercised in its proper sphere. Use what you have been given.

Giving (metadidōmi, μεταδίδωμι) should be generous (haplotēs, ἁπλότης, simplicity, sincerity). Leading (proistēmi, προΐστημι) requires diligence (spoudē, σπουδή, earnestness). Showing mercy (eleōn, ἐλεῶν) should be cheerful (hilarotēs, ἱλαρότης). Manner matters as much as action.
Love is both affection and moral conviction. Hate evil (apostygeō, ἀποστυγέω, abhor). Cling to good (kollaomai, κολλάομαι, glue yourself to). Love discerns between good and evil.
Honor (timē, τιμή) means esteem, value. Prefer others above yourself. This is counter-cultural, anti-pride.

Spiritual fervor (pneumati zeō, πνεύματι ζέω, boiling in spirit) is passionate devotion. Serve the Lord (kyriō douleuō, κυρίῳ δουλεύω, slave for the Lord). Christianity is not passive but active and fervent.
n), patient affliction (endure suffering without complaint), faithful prayer (persevere in intercession). These sustain Christian life through trials.
The Lord's people in need are fellow believers. Practice hospitality (philoxenia, φιλοξενία, love of strangers). Care for the body extends beyond preferences.
Bless (eulogeō, εὐλογέω, speak well of) persecutors. Do not curse (kataraomai, καταράομαι, invoke evil on). This is supernatural love.
Rejoice with rejoicers (share joy, not envy). Mourn with mourners (share grief, not indifference). This is emotional solidarity.
Not proud (hypsēla, ὑψηλά, high things). Associate with the lowly (tapeinois, ταπεινοῖς). Not conceited (phronimoi, φρόνιμοι, wise in your own eyes). Humility overcomes class and status.
Evil for evil is forbidden. Be careful (pronoeomai, προνοέομαι, think ahead) to do what is right publicly. Christian conduct should be above reproach before all.
If possible recognizes it is not always achievable. As far as it depends on you limits responsibility to what you control. We pursue peace but cannot force it.
Vengeance belongs to God, not us. Leave room (dote topon, δότε τόπον, give place) means step aside and let God judge. Trust God's justice rather than taking matters into your own hands.
Overcome evil with good. Feeding enemies is radical love. Burning coals may refer to shame leading to repentance, or unexpected kindness that melts hostility. Either way, love defeats hatred.
uer evil with good. This is the way of the cross. Evil is defeated not by more evil but by good. Christian love transforms enemies.
